criteria.label: Given an ML EDA question, name the correct chart family and justify from variable types.
criteria.example: Question: "Is the target imbalanced?" → bar chart of value_counts(); justification: one categorical variable, comparing category frequencies.

criteria.label: Distinguish when to use line vs bar for metrics across groups or time.
criteria.example: Train/val loss per epoch → line (ordered x). Accuracy of three models with no order → bar. Explain that lines encode progression.

criteria.label: Select histogram vs box vs scatter for univariate and bivariate numeric questions.
criteria.example: One feature skew → histogram. Feature X by class label spread → box. Two numeric features relationship → scatter.

criteria.label: Explain scope: what this topic owns vs Matplotlib, Seaborn, Plotly, and Common distributions.
criteria.example: State that chart-family choice is here; library APIs are downstream; histogram interpretation links to Common distributions without re-deriving PDFs.

criteria.label: Design a minimal EDA chart plan for a new tabular classification dataset.
criteria.example: List: bar of target counts; histogram grid for numeric features; heatmap of df.corr(); scatter for top correlated pair—each with one-sentence question mapping.
